items.hook: A novel is an extended work of narrative fiction usually written in prose and published as a book.
items.body: The word derives from the Italian: novella for 'new', 'news', or 'short story ', itself from the Latin: novella, a singular noun use of the neuter plural of novellus, diminutive of novus, meaning 'new'. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Can you spot the poacher’s handprint? Earth Photo award winners – in pictures
items.body: From scientific tricks to stop turtle traffickers to stranded seals and displaced workers, these images all scooped prizes at this year’s Earth Photo awards

items.hook: Central High School, later known as Central Colored School, (1917–1949) was a public high school for African American students in Shreveport, Louisiana.
items.body: It was the first and the only public high school for African American students in the city of Shreveport during its years of operations, 1917 until 1949. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Joan of Arc, taken by the Burgundians in May, is handed over to Pierre Cauchon, the bishop of Beauvais.
items.body: Joan of Arc is a patron saint of France, honored as a defender of the French nation for her role in the siege of Orléans and her insistence on the coronation of Charles VII of France during the Hundred Years' War. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Daniele Luchetti shooting Dove non mi hai portata, starring Tecla Insolia and Margherita Buy - Production / Funding - Italy
items.body: Adapted from Maria Grazia Calandrone’s Strega Prize-shortlisted novel, the film is an Italian-Spanish co-production between Fandango, Vision Distribution and BTeam Prods

items.hook: They Fight review – boxing drama is an emotional gutpunch
items.body:
Anchored by an indelible André Holland performance, the film finds tenderness and warmth amid its gritty Washington DC backdrop

In the lineage of

items.hook: Nicolas Boukhrief shooting 14 juillet - Production / Funding - France/Belgium
items.body: Alexandra Lamy, Camille Rutherford and Jonathan Zaccaï are starring in the filmmaker’s new movie, produced by Bizibi and Dimanche Soir, and set to be sold by The Bureau Sales

items.hook: Argoman the Fantastic Superman is a 1967 Italian superhero Eurospy film directed by Sergio Grieco, and starring Roger Browne, Dominique Boschero, and Eduardo Fajardo.
items.body: It was released by Fida Cinematografica on March 24, 1967. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In the 14 July Revolution in Iraq, the monarchy is overthrown by popular forces led by Abd al-Karim Qasim, who becomes the nation's new leader.
items.body: The 14 July Revolution, also known as the 1958 Iraqi military coup, was a military coup that took place on 14 July 1958 in Iraq, resulting in the toppling of King Faisal II and the overthrow of the Hashemite-led Kingdom of Iraq. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Marius Olteanu is in post-production on sophomore feature We Won't Get Old Together - Production / Funding - Romania/France
items.body: The Romanian-French co-production follows a gay architect whose life unravels after an unexpected death

items.hook: The first ascent of the Matterhorn is completed by Edward Whymper and his party, four of whom die on the descent.
items.body: The first ascent of the Matterhorn was a mountaineering expedition of the Matterhorn made by Edward Whymper, Lord Francis Douglas, Charles Hudson, Douglas Hadow, Michel Croz, and two Zermatt guides, Peter Taugwalder and his son of the same name, on 14 July 1865. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: August von Eisenhart was a Bavarian lawyer who became a politician.
items.body: Between 1870 and 1876 he served as the king's cabinet secretary. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Gracie Abrams: Daughter from Hell review – bloodless anthems hit like a faceful of icing sugar
items.body:
(Interscope)
Despite their goth-coded attempts at emotional turbulence, the saccharine songs of Abrams’ third album feel adolescent in their melodrama

Gracie Abrams’ third album is a full-blown crime scene. Acro

items.hook: Rus Flight 9633 crashes during takeoff from Chkalovsky Airport, killing all 10 people on board.
items.body: Rus Flight 9633 refers to the crash involving an IL-76TD aircraft operated by Rus Airlines from Chkalovsky Airport to Taiyuan Wusu Airport with intermediate stops at Alykel Airport and Bratsk Airport. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: ‘Fun, propulsive, full of queer joy’: readers’ favourite albums of 2026 so far, from Muna to Raye and J Cole
items.body:
After the Guardian’s music critics chose their best of the half-year, we asked you for your picks – from Brian Jackson and Arlo Parks to Maya Hawke, Flea and more

•

items.hook: King Otto II and his Frankish army are defeated by the Muslim army of al-Qasim at Cape Colonna, Southern Italy.
items.body: Otto II, called the Red, was Holy Roman Emperor from 973 until his death in 983. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In music, metre or meter refers to regularly recurring patterns and accents such as downbeats and beats.
items.body: Unlike rhythm, metric onsets are not necessarily sounded, but are nevertheless implied by the performer and expected by the listener. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Jane Goodall arrives at the Gombe Stream Reserve in present-day Tanzania to begin her study of chimpanzees in the wild.
items.body: Dame Valerie Jane Morris Goodall was an English primatologist and anthropologist. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
